Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»BREAKING: Tatjana Smith settles for silver for Team South Africa

Tatjana Smith won the silver medal at the Paris Olympics 2024 after she narrowly failed to defend her Olympic title in the 200m breaststroke.

Tatjana Smithwon the silver medal at theParis Olympics 2024after she narrowly failed to defend her Olympic title in the women’s200m breaststrokeon Thursday night.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Having won the 100m breaststroke gold medal earlier in the week, Smith was runner-up to AmericanKate Douglassin the four-length race at the Paris La Défense Arena.

Smith stopped the clock in a time of 2:19:60, with Douglass winning in a time of 2:19:24.

Meanwhile, South Africa’sKaylene Corbertfinished seventh in 2:24:46.

The 27-year-old Johannesburg-born star previously won the 200m breaststroke at the delayed 2020 Tokyo Olympics.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

The silver was Tatjana Smith’s fourth medal in her Olympic career. She remains tied with Charles Winslow, Chad le Clos and Caster Semenya on two gold medals.

title: BREAKING: Tatjana Smith settles for silver for Team South Africa
author: Garrin Lambley
description: Tatjana Smith won the silver medal of the Paris Olympics 2024 after she narrowly failed to defend her Olympic title in the 200m breaststroke.
